title: Rebuild as a single closed_loop.py module
body: Replace the current multi-stage pipeline (extract_*.py → calibrate_weights.py → align_parallel.py → gap_analysis.py → validate_against_comments.py → write_reports.py) with a single module scripts/closed_loop.py exposing State and step(state) → state'. Iteration to fixed point. Existing scripts/ files remain as prior-run artifacts for comparison but are not called by the new loop.

title: Atoms start with τ = σ (symmetric at grade 1)
body: For atomic observations derived from verbatim parse-tree kind strings, τ_K(thing) = σ_K(thing) = 'kind K appears in thing'. No asymmetric channel is imposed at the atomic level. Asymmetry emerges ONLY via residue-driven wedge articulation at higher grades, where K_i ∧ K_j takes τ from one parent and σ from the other by the exterior-algebra combinator.

title: Things = named declarations, not every parse-tree node
body: For this first-pass rebuild, 'things' (the domain of alignment) are top-level named declarations across all sources: pandoc Div.{definition,theorem,...}, agda module/function/record/data/postulate, python FunctionDef/ClassDef/Module/Assign. Their atomic observations are every kind string appearing in their subtree. Using every parse-tree node would scale O(n²) in pair comparison to hundreds-of-thousands squared. Can be relaxed later if residue analysis suggests finer granularity matters.

title: Initial atoms = verbatim kind strings from subtrees
body: The initial K pool is the set of kind strings observed anywhere in any thing's subtree. No source prefix, no lowercasing, no canonicalization. 'Module' and 'module' are different K's; 'FunctionDef' and 'function' are different K's. Any correspondence between them must emerge via wedge discovery across co-firing residue patterns.

title: No commit/tier/triple output
body: The pipeline does not output a filtered set of committed triples. It outputs the full Belnap state: every K in the pool with provenance and firing distribution; every thing-pair's four-cell profile under every K-pair; a highlight of which (pair, K-pair) combinations are Boolean-earned; a trajectory log of κ-articulations. Reports are pretty-printed views of that state.

title: Wedge K fires iff both parents fire (AND semantics for profile inclusion)
body: When a wedge K_i ∧ K_j is articulated, its firing predicate is K_i(thing) AND K_j(thing). This is the indicator function for the grade-2 element being in the thing's profile. The exterior algebra combinator for τ/σ of the wedge (τ_A·σ_B + σ_A·τ_B in GF(2)) is a separate question handled when grade-2 chirality semantics are switched on — initially the wedge's τ = σ = the AND of parents.

title: Termination = no productive wedge articulation
body: step() returns a state; iteration compares state.pool to previous state.pool. When no new wedges are articulated AND weights have stabilized below an epsilon, loop terminates. No iteration counter, no max steps. Termination is a property of the state evolution, not an external schedule.

title: Citation oracle calibrates weights, does not gate commits
body: The authorial citation data (agda/python docstrings referencing paper definitions) is used only to tune per-K weights — specifically, weights adjust in the direction that increases Belnap evidence on oracle pairs. The oracle never participates in the commit/reject decision (because there is no such decision). Validation against the oracle is a post-hoc query of the final state, not a pipeline stage.
